title: picea abies f. cincinnata
intro: Picea abies f. cincinnata, also known as the Weeping Norway Spruce, is a cultivar of the Norway Spruce tree (Picea abies), characterized by its unique weeping branches and compact growth habit. The Weeping Norway Spruce is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is also valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, the Weeping Norway Spruce is prepared for medicinal use through steam distillation, which extracts its essential oil, or by drying the leaves and stems to create a herbal tea or tincture.
